focus: Create a reusable cached retrieval-pool workflow so reranker tuning can run offline and remote OpenSearch is reserved for cache refreshes and final validation.
reason_started: The operator asked whether local OpenSearch-style testing could speed up experimentation before implementing only the best approaches on the remote OpenSearch-backed path.
reason_ended: A field-sum top-50 retrieval cache was generated from live OpenSearch and the PRF phrase-coherence tuner reproduced the current best nDCG@10 result offline from that cache.

validators_executed.name: npm run cache:cranfield
validators_executed.evidence: Generated a reusable live OpenSearch field-sum top-50 retrieval cache for all 225 Cranfield queries.

validators_executed.name: npm run tune:cranfield:prf -- --retrieval-cache
validators_executed.evidence: Offline tuning from the retrieval cache reproduced the refined PRF result: nDCG@10 0.3260 with phraseWeight 0.01.

validators_executed.name: npm run validate
validators_executed.evidence: 32 node tests passed; MDE parser, strengthened traceability validation, fixture index check, and fixture evaluation passed. Final run is recorded in VAL-027.

validators_skipped.name: Cloudflare deploy verification
validators_skipped.reason: No deployment was requested; GEN-017 changes local evaluation workflow and leaves public/default behavior unchanged.

validators_skipped.name: remote final candidate evaluation
validators_skipped.reason: No new ranking behavior was selected; the cached tuner only reproduced the GEN-016 refined PRF result.

evidence_quality_notes: GEN-017 improves experiment speed and reproducibility rather than raising the candidate metric.
Cached pools must be refreshed when the index, analyzer, first-stage architecture, query set, or retrieve depth changes.
The public/default architecture remains ARCH-0.1 and refined PRF remains an ARCH-0.2-candidate pending transferability evidence.
